page: Amazon Web Services c8g.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the custom 64-bit ARM-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores with 48 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz without hyperthreading. The memory configuration consists of 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. It does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. The instance achieved a top-tier rating in Geekbench multi-core execution and software compilation using Clang. Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier capabilities, with large-block read speeds reaching 395.06 GB/sec and write speeds at 184.52 GB/sec. In database operations, the instance scored in the top tier on Passmark database tests and demonstrated strong Redis SET performance at 98.88 million operations per second. Single-core performance, such as stress-ng single-core and Passmark CPU tests, remains in the average tier.

The arm64 architecture and dedicated core allocation deliver high resource density and memory bandwidth efficiency, making it a cost-effective option for highly parallelized workloads compared to traditional x86 alternatives. However, the lack of local storage and GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy local data caching or hardware-accelerated machine learning. The instance is best suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded data decompression.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of RAM, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and static web serving capabilities. Conversely, single-core performance and cryptographic operations align with average tiers. This hardware profile offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized tasks. It is highly suitable for workloads such as software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 48 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 96 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for highly parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM and 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core software compilation performance
Strong database operations and static web serving throughput
No local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration included
Optimized for parallel computing, software builds, and web serving
